violations.text: Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2025

Food contact surfaces of the bandsaw were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. During time of inspection, staff stated bandsaw was not cleaned in a few days. Ambient of walk-in cooler was observed at 33 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Meat cutting equipment in walk-in cooler must be cleaned every 24 hours. PIC informed and bandsaw was cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2025

The cutting boards in the meat cutting area and front deli area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 01/11/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets (2 door retail reach-in) were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2026

The drain plugs for the outside dumpsters are missing. Drains in receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must have drain plugs in place. New Violation. Correct By: 01/11/2026

The fan guards in the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2025

The floors in the walk-in cooler are constructed of cement which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2025

Cold-holding units (Deli Display Cases) were observed in poor repair in the front prep area (glass doors observed in poor repair, tape observed in various spots). Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications. New Violation. Correct By: 12/21/2024

1. Several floor tiles by the 3 bay sink and prep table were observed in poor repair 2. Coving observed in poor repair in walk-in cooler 3. Portion of ceiling in storage room observed in poor repair (chipped paint and water damage) New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2024

The floors in the walk-in cooler are constructed of cement which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2025

Raw animal foods such as eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as cookies, beverages and olives in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024

Various deli meats and cheeses were observed held between 45-47 degrees Fahrenheit, unit was observed at an ambient temperature of 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed during time of inspection; foods that were out of temperature for an unknown amount of time was voluntarily disposed of. Deli case was taken out of service during time of inspection to hold TCS/PHF foods. Equipment to be properly adjusted/repaired/ replaced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024

Packaged food items and single service items were observed stored on the floor in the retail space and behind counter in deli space. Food and single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024

Cold-holding units (Deli Display Cases) were observed in poor repair in the front prep area (glass doors observed in poor repair, tape observed in various spots). Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/21/2023

1. Several floor tiles by the 3 bay sink and prep table were observed in poor repair 2. Coving observed in poor repair in walk-in cooler 3. Portion of ceiling in storage room observed in poor repair (chipped paint and water damage) New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2024

The floors in the walk-in cooler are constructed of cement which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. -Staff removed the open beverage container during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. -Staff washed, rinsed, and sanitized the slicer during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

Cold- holding units (Deli Display Case) were observed in poor repair in the front prep area. Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications. New Violation. Correct By: 12/21/2023

The ceiling in the front prep area and ceiling in the 3 bay sink area are covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Ceiling tiles shall be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2024
